title: Ram Ratan vs Durjan Singh on 12 November, 2014
body: Ram Ratan vs Durjan Singh on 12 November, 2014 S.A. No.72/2006 (Ram Ratan Vs. Durjan Singh & Others) 12.11.2014 Shri Anvesh Jain, Advocate for the appellant. Shri Sunil Kumar Jain, Advocate for the respondent. This second appeal u/S 100 of C.P.C. 1908 has been filed against the judgment and decree dated 16.11.2005 passed by the IV Upper District Judge(Fast Track), District Shivpuri in Civil Appeal No. 187/2005, by which the judgment and decree dated 06.09.2005 passed by the Civil Judge Class II, Kolaras, Distt. Shivpuri in Civil suit no. 7A/2005 has been reversed and decreed the suit of the plaintiffs. A short point is involved in this case, as to whether the Civil Court was justified in interfering with the order of Revenue authorities u/S 116 of the M.P. Land Revenue Code, 1959 and consequently the first Appellate Court having reversed the said order was correct. With the consent of parties, arguments are heard in the matter. Attention of this Court is drawn to Section 257 of the MPLR Code 1959 which provides jurisdiction of the Civil Court with respect to an entry passed by the Revenue Authorities u/S 116 of MPLR Code. In the present case, the respondent instead of S.A. No.72/2006 challenging the entries in the Appellate Court i.e. before the SDO and thereafter, not satisfied before the Board of Revenue thought to file a civil suit. The suit was allowed in favour of the respondents but the first Appellate Court reversed the judgment. The counsel appearing in this second appeal submits that proceedings undertaken by the Civil Court i.e. by the Civil Judge and as well as the First Appellate Court was illegal and void in view of provision of Section 257(f) of the MP Land Revenue Code which reads as undertaken: "Except as otherwise provided in this Code, or in any other enactment for the time being in force, no Civil Court shall entertain any suit instituted or application made to obtain a decision or order on any matter which the State Government, the Board, or any Revenue Officer is by this Code, empowered to determine, decide or dispose of, and in particular and without prejudice to the generality of this provision, no Civil Court shall exercise jurisdiction over any of the following matters: f. any claim against the State Government to have any entry made in any land record or to have any such entry omitted or amended. A bare reading of the aforesaid provision shows that revenue entries with respect to correction in revenue records u/S 116 of M.P. Land Revenue Code could not have been challenged before the Civil Court and therefore S.A. No.72/2006 the civil suit filed before the Civil Judge, the appeal filed before the First Appellate Court was unjustified. Therefore this second appeal is decided by holding that, the proceedings undertaken by the parties before the Civil Court/First Appellate Court with respect to correction of entires u/S 116 of the MPLR Code were illegal and void. In view of the aforesaid, parties are held entitled to challenge the entries, in case they are aggrieved by the entries before the revenue authorities. The judgment of the First Appellate Court and as well as the Civil Court are set aside. With these observation, this appeal is dismissed. No order as to cost. (M.C. Garg) Judge sh/-
